channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump asks Supreme Court to delay TikTok ban so he can weigh in after he takes office
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump asked the Supreme Court on Friday to pause the potential TikTok ban from going into effect until his administration can pursue a “political resolution” to the issue.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Sportscasting icon Greg Gumbel dies at 78
item.description: Sportscaster Greg Gumbel, best known for his long career with CBS Sports, has died Friday, Dec. 27, according to CBS Sports and Gumbel's family.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Azerbaijani and U.S. officials suggest plane that crashed may have been hit by weapons fire
item.description: Russian air defense systems may have brought down an Azerbaijani airliner this week, a U.S. official said Friday after an Azerbaijani minister also suggested the plane was hit by a weapon, citing expert analysis and survivor accounts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: US homelessness up double digits, rising numbers of asylum seekers, affordability crisis among causes
item.description: A report from the U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development shows homelessness rose 18% in 2024, with causes including asylum seekers, lack of affordable housing and natural disasters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US homelessness up 18% as affordable housing remains out of reach for many people
item.description: The United States saw an 18.1% increase in homelessness this year, a dramatic rise driven mostly by a lack of affordable housing as well as devastating natural disasters and a surge of migrants in several parts of the country, federal officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Another unticketed passenger attempts to stow away on a Delta flight
item.description: In the thick of holiday travel, another unticketed passenger attempted to stow away on a Delta Air Lines flight.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Pennsylvania teacher jailed in Russia formally designated as wrongfully detained
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An American schoolteacher arrested in Russia on drug charges more than four years ago has been designated by the U.S. government as wrongfully detained, the State Department said Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bird flu virus in Louisiana patient likely mutated to be more transmissible, CDC says
item.description: The agency, in its latest update released Thursday, said the mutations were not found in samples taken from wild birds, suggesting the virus changed over the course of the patient’s infection.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KRON4

item.title: New Mega Millions jackpot stands at $1.22B after no winner during Christmas Eve drawing
item.description: The Mega Millions jackpot continues to grow after no ticket matched all six numbers drawn on Christmas Eve. The new estimated jackpot for Friday night’s drawing is $1.22 billion, California Lottery officials announced in a post on X. This is the fifth largest jackpot in the game's history.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: China sanctions 7 companies over US military assistance to Taiwan
item.description: The sanctions also come in response to the recent approval of the U.S. government's annual defense spending bill, which a Chinese Foreign Ministry statement said “includes multiple negative sections on China.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Mexico tests app for migrants to send alert if they're about to be detained in US
item.description: U.S. authorities are obliged to give notice to home-country consulates when a foreign citizen is detained. Mexico says it has beefed up consular staff and legal aid to help migrants in the legal process related to deportation.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: How Trump changed America’s legal landscape
item.description: President-elect Trump's legal issues dominated his 2024 presidential bid, where courtroom appearances were just as common as rallies and campaign stops.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man indicted in burning death of woman inside New York City subway train: prosecutors
item.description: The indictment comes days after Sebastian Zapeta’s arrest and subsequent police questioning, in which authorities say he claimed not to know what had happened but identified himself in photos and surveillance video showing the fire being lit.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: OpenAI whistleblower's mother wants suicide death investigation reopened
item.description: OpenAI whistleblower Suchir Balaji's death was ruled a suicide — but his family is calling on police to reopen their investigation, saying it "doesn't look like a normal situation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former NCAA quarterback Tommy Lazzaro dies in 'tragic' accident at US Air Force base
item.description: Tommy Lazzaro, an Army sergeant and former quarterback for the Central Michigan Chippewas, has died following an accident at Eglin Air Force Base in Florida. He was 27.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Illegal migrant indicted on murder charges after woman is burned to death on subway
item.description: The illegal migrant accused of burning a New York City subway rider to death on Sunday has been charged with murder and other crimes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Most blame insurance profits and denials alongside the killer in UHC CEO death: poll
item.description: Most Americans believe health insurance profits and coverage denials share responsibility for the killing of UnitedHealthcare's CEO — although not as much as the person who pulled the trigger, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Blue state's experiment collapses after a decade and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New 2025 laws hit hot topics from AI in movies to rapid-fire guns
item.description: New laws taking effect with the new year will affect Hollywood actors, social media stars and chatty motorists.
item.dc:creator: David A. Lieb, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Man arrested for disrupting Christmas church services, pouring whiskey into holy water: deputies
item.description: Authorities in Maryland said a man has been arrested after he disrupted services at two local churches on Christmas Eve and endangered citizens.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Getting too close to first responders will soon be a crime in Florida
item.description: Florida will soon have a so-called “Halo Law” meant to protect first responders. The new law, starting in January, creates a 25 feet buffer zone around law enforcement officers, firefighters, and emergency medical crews.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, NewsNation

item.title: Richard Parsons, prominent executive who led Time Warner and Citigroup, dies at 76
item.description: Richard Parsons, one of corporate America’s most prominent Black executives who held top posts at Time Warner and Citigroup, has died.
item.dc:creator: Anick Jesdanun And Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Biden signs a bill officially making the bald eagle the national bird of the US
item.description: The bald eagle, a symbol of the power and strength of the United States for more than 240 years, earned an overdue honor on Tuesday: It officially became the country's national bird.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Opinion: Elon Musk wants to ‘delete’ many Americans' financial lifeline
item.description: It is especially surprising that one of the first federal agencies to come under scrutiny from the incoming administration is one that has returned billions of dollars to many of the same consumers who were counting on leaders in Washington to look out for their wallets.
item.dc:creator: Sharon McGowan, opinion contributor, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Eye drops recalled nationwide due to fungal contamination
item.description: Check your medicine cabinets: A brand of eye drops sold nationally has been recalled due to the presence of fungus, which may cause vision damage.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Self, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bill Clinton discharged after hospitalization for flu
item.description: Former President Clinton was discharged Tuesday morning after being hospitalized a day earlier for flu, according to his spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Seafarers move global trade
item.description: BARCELONA, Spain (AP) — On a balmy December evening, four Indian crew members, eager to go explore this Mediterranean city where their container ship docked for a few hours, turned for help to Stella Maris.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL’ORTO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Walmart illegally opened bank accounts for 1 million drivers, lawsuit alleges
item.description: Walmart and fintech company Branch Messenger are being sued by the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau for allegedly forcing more than one million delivery workers to use expensive deposit accounts to access their paychecks.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, ABC 4

item.title: Fire alarm scare forces evacuations from Eiffel Tower
item.description: The evacuation came less than a month after one of Paris' other signature landmarks, Notre Dame Cathedral, reopened following a devastating 2019 fire.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, Fox 5

item.title: Man faces murder charges in the death of a woman who was lit on fire in New York City subway
item.description: A man is facing murder and arson charges in New York City for allegedly setting a woman on fire inside a subway train and then watching her die after she was engulfed in flames, police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE and SUSAN HAIGH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: WATCH: Owl flies into Virginia home, lands atop Christmas tree
item.description: One family in Virginia got quite the surprise on Wednesday, Dec. 18, when a barred owl flew into their home through the chimney and ended up perched on their Christmas tree.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Relief, defiance, anger: Families and advocates react to Biden's death row commutations
item.description: COLUMBIA, South Carolina (AP) — Victims' families and others affected by crimes that resulted in federal death row convictions shared a range of emotions on Monday, from relief to anger, after President Joe Biden commuted dozens of the sentences.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS and ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Inside the state and federal charges against the man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare’s CEO
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A triptych of criminal charges paints a searing, sometimes disparate portrait of the man accused of ambushing and killing UnitedHealthcare’s CEO Brian Thompson as the executive arrived at a Manhattan hotel for his company’s annual investor conference.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump wants mass deportations. A ride-along with immigration officers shows the challenges
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The immigration officers sat in their vehicles before dawn near a two-story building. A New York subway line rumbled overhead, then an officer's voice crackled over the radio. After watching for about two hours, he said, “I think that's Tango,” using a term for target. “Gray hoodie.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Civil rights icon James Meredith is honored in his Mississippi hometown
item.description: KOSCIUSKO, Miss. (AP) — A new historical marker has been unveiled in the hometown of James Meredith, honoring the Black man who fought white supremacy by integrating the University of Mississippi in 1962.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, ABC 4
